title: Toasted Thai Chicken Casserole
description: A hearty and aromatic Thai-inspired casserole that combines the flavors of wholegrain mustard and toasted spices with tender chicken and crunchy vegetables.
instructions: In a large bowl, toss the chicken with 1 tablespoon of coconut oil, 1 tablespoon of wholegrain mustard, and a pinch of salt and pepper until the chicken is evenly coated.
Heat the remaining 1 tablespoon of coconut oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat. Add the chicken and cook until browned and cooked through, about 5-6 minutes.
Transfer the cooked chicken to a plate and set aside.
In the same skillet, add the sliced onion and cook until softened, about 3-4 minutes.
Add the minced garlic and cook for an additional 1 minute.
Stir in the chopped Thai basil, fish sauce, and a pinch of salt and pepper. Cook for 1 minute.
Add the sliced red bell pepper and cook until tender, about 3-4 minutes.
In a large bowl, combine the cooked chicken, the skillet mixture, and the toasted peanuts.
Transfer the mixture to a 9x13 inch baking dish and set aside.
In a small bowl, whisk together the remaining 1 tablespoon of wholegrain mustard and 2 tablespoons of coconut oil.
Pour the mustard-coconut oil mixture evenly over the top of the casserole.
Bake the casserole in the preheated oven for 15-20 minutes, or until the top is golden brown and the casserole is heated through.
Remove the casserole from the oven and let it cool for 5 minutes before serving.
